    88→# ==============================================================================
    89→# ISSUE DETECTION (Lightweight checks - full agents run separately)
    90→# ==============================================================================
    91→
    92→class IssueDetector:
    93→    """Detect common issues for quality scoring."""
    94→
    95→    @staticmethod
    96→    def check_quarto_compilation(filepath: Path) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    97→        """Check if Quarto file compiles successfully."""
    98→        try:
    99→            result = subprocess.run(
   100→                ['quarto', 'render', str(filepath), '--to', 'html'],
   101→                capture_output=True,
   102→                text=True,
   103→                timeout=120,
   104→                cwd=filepath.parent
   105→            )
   106→            if result.returncode != 0:
   107→                return False, result.stderr
   108→            return True, ""
   109→        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
   110→            return False, "Compilation timeout (>2min)"
   111→        except FileNotFoundError:
   112→            return False, "Quarto not installed"
   113→
   114→    @staticmethod
   115→    def check_equation_overflow(content: str) -> List[int]:
   116→        """Detect displayed equations with single lines likely to overflow.
   117→
   118→        Flags equations only when a SINGLE LINE within a math block exceeds
   119→        120 characters. Multi-line equations properly broken across lines
   120→        are not flagged even if the total block is long.
   121→
   122→        Checks:
   123→        - $$ ... $$ blocks (Quarto/LaTeX)
   124→        - \\begin{equation} ... \\end{equation} blocks
   125→        - \\begin{align} ... \\end{align} blocks
   126→        - \\begin{gather} ... \\end{gather} blocks
   127→        """
   128→        overflows = []
   129→        lines = content.split('\n')
   130→        in_math = False
   131→        math_start = 0
   132→        math_delim = None  # Track which delimiter opened the block
   133→
   134→        for i, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   135→            stripped = line.strip()
   136→
   137→            # Check for $$ delimiter (toggle)
   138→            if '$$' in stripped and math_delim != 'env':
   139→                if not in_math:
   140→                    in_math = True
   141→                    math_start = i
   142→                    math_delim = '$$'
   143→                    # Handle single-line $$ ... $$ (both delimiters on same line)
   144→                    if stripped.count('$$') >= 2:
   145→                        inner = stripped.split('$$')[1]
   146→                        if len(inner.strip()) > 120:
   147→                            overflows.append(i)
   148→                        in_math = False
   149→                        math_delim = None
   150→                    continue
   151→                else:
   152→                    in_math = False
   153→                    math_delim = None
   154→                    continue
   155→
   156→            # Check for \begin{equation/align/gather/...}
   157→            env_begin = re.match(
   158→                r'\\begin\{(equation|align|gather|multline|eqnarray)\*?\}', stripped
   159→            )
   160→            if env_begin and not in_math:
   161→                in_math = True
   162→                math_start = i
   163→                math_delim = 'env'
   164→                continue
   165→
   166→            # Check for \end{equation/align/gather/...}
   167→            if re.match(r'\\end\{(equation|align|gather|multline|eqnarray)\*?\}', stripped):
   168→                in_math = False
   169→                math_delim = None
   170→                continue
   171→
   172→            # Inside a math block: check individual line length
   173→            if in_math:
   174→                # Strip LaTeX comments before measuring
   175→                code_part = line.split('%')[0] if '%' in line else line
   176→                if len(code_part.strip()) > 120:
   177→                    overflows.append(i)
   178→
   179→        return overflows
   180→
   181→    @staticmethod
   182→    def check_broken_citations(content: str, bib_file: Path) -> List[str]:
   183→        """Check for LaTeX citation keys not in bibliography.
   184→
   185→        Matches \\cite{}, \\citep{}, \\citet{}, \\citeauthor{}, \\citeyear{}, etc.
   186→        """
   187→        cite_pattern = r'\\cite[a-z]*\{([^}]+)\}'
   188→        cited_keys = set()
   189→        for match in re.finditer(cite_pattern, content):
   190→            keys = match.group(1).split(',')
   191→            cited_keys.update(k.strip() for k in keys)
   192→
   193→        if not bib_file.exists():
   194→            return list(cited_keys)
   195→
   196→        bib_content = bib_file.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
   197→        bib_keys = set(re.findall(r'@\w+\{([^,]+),', bib_content))
   198→
   199→        broken = cited_keys - bib_keys
   200→        return list(broken)
   201→
   202→    @staticmethod
   203→    def check_plotly_widgets(html_file: Path, expected: int = None) -> Tuple[int, bool]:
   204→        """Check if plotly charts rendered in HTML."""
   205→        if not html_file.exists():
   206→            return 0, False
   207→
   208→        html_content = html_file.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
   209→        actual_count = html_content.count('htmlwidget')
   210→
   211→        if expected is None:
   212→            return actual_count, True
   213→
   214→        return actual_count, (actual_count >= expected)
   215→
   216→    @staticmethod
   217→    def check_r_syntax(filepath: Path) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
   218→        """Check R script for syntax errors."""
   219→        try:
   220→            result = subprocess.run(
   221→                ['Rscript', '-e', f'parse("{filepath}")'],
   222→                capture_output=True,
   223→                text=True,
   224→                timeout=10
   225→            )
   226→            if result.returncode != 0:
   227→                return False, result.stderr
   228→            return True, ""
   229→        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
   230→            return False, "Syntax check timeout"
   231→        except FileNotFoundError:
   232→            return False, "Rscript not installed"
   233→
   234→    @staticmethod
   235→    def check_hardcoded_paths(content: str) -> List[int]:
   236→        """Detect absolute paths in R scripts."""
   237→        issues = []
   238→        lines = content.split('\n')
   239→
   240→        for i, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   241→            if re.search(r'["\'][/\\]|["\'][A-Za-z]:[/\\]', line):
   242→                if not re.search(r'http:|https:|file://|/tmp/', line):
   243→                    issues.append(i)
   244→
   245→        return issues
   246→
   247→    @staticmethod
   248→    def check_latex_syntax(content: str) -> List[Dict]:
   249→        """Check for common LaTeX syntax issues without compiling.
   250→
   251→        Looks for:
   252→        - Unmatched braces
   253→        - Unclosed environments
   254→        - Common typos in commands
   255→        """
   256→        issues = []
   257→        lines = content.split('\n')
   258→
   259→        # Track open environments
   260→        env_stack = []
   261→        for i, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   262→            # Skip comments
   263→            stripped = line.split('%')[0] if '%' in line else line
   264→
   265→            # Check for \begin{env}
   266→            for match in re.finditer(r'\\begin\{(\w+)\}', stripped):
   267→                env_stack.append((match.group(1), i))
   268→
   269→            # Check for \end{env}
   270→            for match in re.finditer(r'\\end\{(\w+)\}', stripped):
   271→                env_name = match.group(1)
   272→                if env_stack and env_stack[-1][0] == env_name:
   273→                    env_stack.pop()
   274→                elif env_stack:
   275→                    issues.append({
   276→                        'line': i,
   277→                        'description': f'Mismatched environment: \\end{{{env_name}}} '
   278→                                       f'but expected \\end{{{env_stack[-1][0]}}} '
   279→                                       f'(opened at line {env_stack[-1][1]})',
   280→                    })
   281→                else:
   282→                    issues.append({
   283→                        'line': i,
   284→                        'description': f'\\end{{{env_name}}} without matching \\begin',
   285→                    })
   286→
   287→        # Report unclosed environments
   288→        for env_name, line_num in env_stack:
   289→            issues.append({
   290→                'line': line_num,
   291→                'description': f'Unclosed environment: \\begin{{{env_name}}} never closed',
   292→            })
   293→
   294→        return issues
   295→
   296→    @staticmethod
   297→    def check_overfull_hbox_risk(content: str) -> List[int]:
   298→        """Detect lines in LaTeX source likely to cause overfull hbox.
   299→
   300→        Checks for very long lines inside text and math environments
   301→        that are likely to overflow the slide width.
   302→        """
   303→        issues = []
   304→        lines = content.split('\n')
   305→        in_frame = False
   306→
   307→        for i, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   308→            stripped = line.split('%')[0] if '%' in line else line
   309→
   310→            # Track frame environments for context
   311→            if r'\begin{frame}' in stripped:
   312→                in_frame = True
   313→            elif r'\end{frame}' in stripped:
   314→                in_frame = False
   315→
   316→            # Flag very long content lines inside frames
   317→            # Strip leading whitespace and LaTeX commands for length check
   318→            if in_frame and len(stripped.strip()) > 120:
   319→                # Skip lines that are just comments or common long commands
   320→                if stripped.strip().startswith('%'):
   321→                    continue
   322→                # Skip includegraphics, input, and similar path-based commands
   323→                if re.match(r'\s*\\(includegraphics|input|bibliography|usepackage)', stripped):
   324→                    continue
   325→                issues.append(i)
   326→
   327→        return issues
   328→
   329→    @staticmethod
   330→    def check_quarto_citations(content: str, bib_file: Path) -> List[str]:
   331→        """Check Quarto-style citation keys against bibliography.
   332→
   333→        Supports patterns: @key, [@key], [@key1; @key2]
   334→        """
   335→        cited_keys = set()
   336→
   337→        # Pattern 1: [@key] or [@key1; @key2; ...]
   338→        bracket_pattern = r'\[([^\]]*@[^\]]+)\]'
   339→        for match in re.finditer(bracket_pattern, content):
   340→            inner = match.group(1)
   341→            # Extract individual @key references from within brackets
   342→            for key_match in re.finditer(r'@([\w:.#$%&\-+?<>~/]+)', inner):
   343→                cited_keys.add(key_match.group(1))
   344→
   345→        # Pattern 2: standalone @key (not inside brackets, not email addresses)
   346→        # Match @key that is preceded by start-of-line or whitespace or punctuation
   347→        # but NOT preceded by characters that indicate an email address
   348→        standalone_pattern = r'(?<![.\w])@([\w:.#$%&\-+?<>~/]+)'
   349→        for match in re.finditer(standalone_pattern, content):
   350→            key = match.group(1)
   351→            # Skip if it looks like a Quarto directive or special syntax
   352→            if key.startswith('{') or key in ('fig', 'tbl', 'sec', 'eq', 'lst'):
   353→                continue
   354→            cited_keys.add(key)
   355→
   356→        if not cited_keys:
   357→            return []
   358→
   359→        if not bib_file.exists():
   360→            return list(cited_keys)
   361→
   362→        bib_content = bib_file.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
   363→        bib_keys = set(re.findall(r'@\w+\{([^,]+),', bib_content))
   364→
   365→        broken = cited_keys - bib_keys
   366→        return list(broken)

   368→# ==============================================================================
   369→# QUALITY SCORER
   370→# ==============================================================================
   371→
   372→class QualityScorer:
   373→    """Calculate quality scores for course materials."""
   374→
   375→    def __init__(self, filepath: Path, verbose: bool = False):
   376→        self.filepath = filepath
   377→        self.verbose = verbose
   378→        self.score = 100
   379→        self.issues = {
   380→            'critical': [],
   381→            'major': [],
   382→            'minor': []
   383→        }
   384→        self.auto_fail = False
   385→
   386→    def score_quarto(self) -> Dict:
   387→        """Score Quarto lecture slides."""
   388→        content = self.filepath.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
   389→
   390→        # Check compilation
   391→        compiles, error = IssueDetector.check_quarto_compilation(self.filepath)
   392→        if not compiles:
   393→            self.auto_fail = True
   394→            self.issues['critical'].append({
   395→                'type': 'compilation_failure',
   396→                'description': 'Quarto compilation failed',
   397→                'details': error[:200],
   398→                'points': 100
   399→            })
   400→            self.score = 0
   401→            return self._generate_report()
   402→
   403→        # Check equation overflow (heuristic)
   404→        equation_overflows = IssueDetector.check_equation_overflow(content)
   405→        for line in equation_overflows:
   406→            self.issues['critical'].append({
   407→                'type': 'equation_overflow',
   408→                'description': f'Potential equation overflow at line {line}',
   409→                'details': 'Single equation line >120 chars may overflow slide',
   410→                'points': 20
   411→            })
   412→            self.score -= 20
   413→
   414→        # Check broken citations (LaTeX-style \cite patterns)
   415→        bib_file = self.filepath.parent.parent / 'Bibliography_base.bib'
   416→        broken_citations = IssueDetector.check_broken_citations(content, bib_file)
   417→
   418→        # Also check Quarto-style @key citations
   419→        quarto_broken = IssueDetector.check_quarto_citations(content, bib_file)
   420→        # Merge both sets, avoiding duplicates
   421→        all_broken = set(broken_citations) | set(quarto_broken)
   422→        for key in all_broken:
   423→            self.issues['critical'].append({
   424→                'type': 'broken_citation',
   425→                'description': f'Citation key not in bibliography: {key}',
   426→                'details': 'Add to Bibliography_base.bib or fix key',
   427→                'points': 15
   428→            })
   429→            self.score -= 15
   430→
   431→        # Check plotly widgets (if HTML exists)
   432→        html_file = self.filepath.parent.parent / 'docs' / 'slides' / self.filepath.with_suffix('.html').name
   433→        if html_file.exists():
   434→            widget_count, _ = IssueDetector.check_plotly_widgets(html_file)
   435→            expected_plotly = content.count('plotly::plot_ly')
   436→            if expected_plotly > 0 and widget_count < expected_plotly:
   437→                missing = expected_plotly - widget_count
   438→                self.issues['critical'].append({
   439→                    'type': 'missing_plotly_chart',
   440→                    'description': f'{missing} plotly chart(s) failed to render',
   441→                    'details': f'Expected {expected_plotly}, found {widget_count}',
   442→                    'points': 10 * missing
   443→                })
   444→                self.score -= 10 * missing
   445→
   446→        self.score = max(0, self.score)
   447→        return self._generate_report()
   448→
   449→    def score_r_script(self) -> Dict:
   450→        """Score R script quality."""
   451→        content = self.filepath.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
   452→
   453→        # Check syntax
   454→        is_valid, error = IssueDetector.check_r_syntax(self.filepath)
   455→        if not is_valid:
   456→            self.auto_fail = True
   457→            self.issues['critical'].append({
   458→                'type': 'syntax_error',
   459→                'description': 'R syntax error',
   460→                'details': error[:200],
   461→                'points': 100
   462→            })
   463→            self.score = 0
   464→            return self._generate_report()
   465→
   466→        # Check hardcoded paths
   467→        path_issues = IssueDetector.check_hardcoded_paths(content)
   468→        for line in path_issues:
   469→            self.issues['critical'].append({
   470→                'type': 'hardcoded_path',
   471→                'description': f'Hardcoded absolute path at line {line}',
   472→                'details': 'Use relative paths or here::here()',
   473→                'points': 20
   474→            })
   475→            self.score -= 20
   476→
   477→        # Check for set.seed() if randomness detected
   478→        has_random = any(fn in content for fn in ['rnorm', 'runif', 'sample', 'rbinom', 'rnbinom'])
   479→        has_seed = 'set.seed' in content
   480→        if has_random and not has_seed:
   481→            self.issues['major'].append({
   482→                'type': 'missing_set_seed',
   483→                'description': 'Missing set.seed() for reproducibility',
   484→                'details': 'Add set.seed(YYYYMMDD) after library() calls',
   485→                'points': 10
   486→            })
   487→            self.score -= 10
   488→
   489→        self.score = max(0, self.score)
   490→        return self._generate_report()
   491→
   492→    def score_beamer(self) -> Dict:
   493→        """Score Beamer/LaTeX lecture slides."""
   494→        content = self.filepath.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
   495→
   496→        # Check for LaTeX syntax issues (without compiling)
   497→        syntax_issues = IssueDetector.check_latex_syntax(content)
   498→        if syntax_issues:
   499→            # Mismatched environments are treated as compilation risk
   500→            for issue in syntax_issues:
   501→                self.issues['critical'].append({
   502→                    'type': 'compilation_failure',
   503→                    'description': f'LaTeX syntax issue at line {issue["line"]}',
   504→                    'details': issue['description'],
   505→                    'points': 100
   506→                })
   507→            self.auto_fail = True
   508→            self.score = 0
   509→            return self._generate_report()
   510→
   511→        # Check for undefined/broken citations (\cite, \citep, \citet patterns)
   512→        bib_file = self.filepath.parent.parent / 'Bibliography_base.bib'
   513→        if not bib_file.exists():
   514→            # Also check same directory
   515→            bib_file = self.filepath.parent / 'Bibliography_base.bib'
   516→        broken_citations = IssueDetector.check_broken_citations(content, bib_file)
   517→        for key in broken_citations:
   518→            self.issues['critical'].append({
   519→                'type': 'undefined_citation',
   520→                'description': f'Citation key not in bibliography: {key}',
   521→                'details': 'Add to Bibliography_base.bib or fix key',
   522→                'points': 15
   523→            })
   524→            self.score -= 15
   525→
   526→        # Check for lines likely to cause overfull hbox
   527→        overfull_lines = IssueDetector.check_overfull_hbox_risk(content)
   528→        for line in overfull_lines:
   529→            self.issues['critical'].append({
   530→                'type': 'overfull_hbox',
   531→                'description': f'Potential overfull hbox at line {line}',
   532→                'details': 'Line >120 chars inside frame may overflow slide width',
   533→                'points': 10
   534→            })
   535→            self.score -= 10
   536→
   537→        # Check equation overflow (same heuristic as Quarto)
   538→        equation_overflows = IssueDetector.check_equation_overflow(content)
   539→        for line_num in equation_overflows:
   540→            self.issues['critical'].append({
   541→                'type': 'overfull_hbox',
   542→                'description': f'Potential equation overflow at line {line_num}',
   543→                'details': 'Single equation line >120 chars likely to overflow',
   544→                'points': 10
   545→            })
   546→            self.score -= 10
   547→
   548→        self.score = max(0, self.score)
   549→        return self._generate_report()
   550→
   551→    def _generate_report(self) -> Dict:
   552→        """Generate quality score report."""
   553→        if self.auto_fail:
   554→            status = 'FAIL'
   555→            threshold = 'None (auto-fail)'
   556→        elif self.score >= THRESHOLDS['excellence']:
   557→            status = 'EXCELLENCE'
   558→            threshold = 'excellence'
   559→        elif self.score >= THRESHOLDS['pr']:
   560→            status = 'PR_READY'
   561→            threshold = 'pr'
   562→        elif self.score >= THRESHOLDS['commit']:
   563→            status = 'COMMIT_READY'
   564→            threshold = 'commit'
   565→        else:
   566→            status = 'BLOCKED'
   567→            threshold = 'None (below commit)'
   568→
   569→        critical_count = len(self.issues['critical'])
   570→        major_count = len(self.issues['major'])
   571→        minor_count = len(self.issues['minor'])
   572→        total_count = critical_count + major_count + minor_count
   573→
   574→        return {
   575→            'filepath': str(self.filepath),
   576→            'score': self.score,
   577→            'status': status,
   578→            'threshold': threshold,
   579→            'auto_fail': self.auto_fail,
   580→            'issues': {
   581→                'critical': self.issues['critical'],
   582→                'major': self.issues['major'],
   583→                'minor': self.issues['minor'],
   584→                'counts': {
   585→                    'critical': critical_count,
   586→                    'major': major_count,
   587→                    'minor': minor_count,
   588→                    'total': total_count
   589→                }
   590→            },
   591→            'thresholds': THRESHOLDS
   592→        }
   593→
   594→    def print_report(self, summary_only: bool = False) -> None:
   595→        """Print formatted quality report."""
   596→        report = self._generate_report()
   597→
   598→        print(f"\n# Quality Score: {self.filepath.name}\n")
   599→
   600→        status_emoji = {
   601→            'EXCELLENCE': '[EXCELLENCE]',
   602→            'PR_READY': '[PASS]',
   603→            'COMMIT_READY': '[PASS]',
   604→            'BLOCKED': '[BLOCKED]',
   605→            'FAIL': '[FAIL]'
   606→        }
   607→
   608→        print(f"## Overall Score: {report['score']}/100 {status_emoji.get(report['status'], '')}")
   609→
   610→        if report['status'] == 'BLOCKED':
   611→            print(f"\n**Status:** BLOCKED - Cannot commit (score < {THRESHOLDS['commit']})")
   612→        elif report['status'] == 'COMMIT_READY':
   613→            print(f"\n**Status:** Ready for commit (score >= {THRESHOLDS['commit']})")
   614→            gap_to_pr = THRESHOLDS['pr'] - report['score']
   615→            print(f"**Next milestone:** PR threshold ({THRESHOLDS['pr']}+)")
   616→            print(f"**Gap analysis:** Need +{gap_to_pr} points to reach PR quality")
   617→        elif report['status'] == 'PR_READY':
   618→            print(f"\n**Status:** Ready for PR (score >= {THRESHOLDS['pr']})")
   619→            gap_to_excellence = THRESHOLDS['excellence'] - report['score']
   620→            if gap_to_excellence > 0:
   621→                print(f"**Next milestone:** Excellence ({THRESHOLDS['excellence']})")
   622→                print(f"**Gap analysis:** +{gap_to_excellence} points to excellence")
   623→        elif report['status'] == 'EXCELLENCE':
   624→            print(f"\n**Status:** Excellence achieved! (score >= {THRESHOLDS['excellence']})")
   625→        elif report['status'] == 'FAIL':
   626→            print(f"\n**Status:** Auto-fail (compilation/syntax error)")
   627→
   628→        if summary_only:
   629→            print(f"\n**Total issues:** {report['issues']['counts']['total']} "
   630→                  f"({report['issues']['counts']['critical']} critical, "
   631→                  f"{report['issues']['counts']['major']} major, "
   632→                  f"{report['issues']['counts']['minor']} minor)")
   633→            return
   634→
   635→        # Detailed issues
   636→        print(f"\n## Critical Issues (MUST FIX): {report['issues']['counts']['critical']}")
   637→        if report['issues']['counts']['critical'] == 0:
   638→            print("No critical issues - safe to commit\n")
   639→        else:
   640→            for i, issue in enumerate(report['issues']['critical'], 1):
   641→                print(f"{i}. **{issue['description']}** (-{issue['points']} points)")
   642→                print(f"   - {issue['details']}\n")
   643→
   644→        if report['issues']['counts']['major'] > 0:
   645→            print(f"## Major Issues (SHOULD FIX): {report['issues']['counts']['major']}")
   646→            for i, issue in enumerate(report['issues']['major'], 1):
   647→                print(f"{i}. **{issue['description']}** (-{issue['points']} points)")
   648→                print(f"   - {issue['details']}\n")
   649→
   650→        if report['issues']['counts']['minor'] > 0 and self.verbose:
   651→            print(f"## Minor Issues (NICE-TO-HAVE): {report['issues']['counts']['minor']}")
   652→            for i, issue in enumerate(report['issues']['minor'], 1):
   653→                print(f"{i}. {issue['description']} (-{issue['points']} points)\n")
   654→
   655→        # Recommendations
   656→        if report['status'] == 'BLOCKED':
   657→            print("## Recommended Actions")
   658→            print("1. Fix all critical issues above")
   659→            print(f"2. Re-run quality score (target: >={THRESHOLDS['commit']})")
   660→            print("3. Commit after reaching threshold\n")
   661→        elif report['status'] == 'COMMIT_READY' and report['score'] < THRESHOLDS['pr']:
   662→            print("## Recommended Actions to Reach PR Threshold")
   663→            points_needed = THRESHOLDS['pr'] - report['score']
   664→            print(f"Need +{points_needed} points to reach {THRESHOLDS['pr']}/100")
   665→            if report['issues']['counts']['major'] > 0:
   666→                print("Fix major issues listed above to improve score")
   667→            print(f"\n**Estimated time:** 10-20 minutes\n")

   673→def main():

   675→        description='Calculate quality scores for course materials',
   676→        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,
   677→        epilog="""
   678→Examples:
   679→  # Score a single Quarto file
   680→  python scripts/quality_score.py Quarto/Lecture6_Topic.qmd
   681→
   682→  # Score multiple files

   690→
   691→  # Summary only (no detailed issues)

   694→  # Verbose output (include minor issues)

   696→
   697→Quality Thresholds:

   743→            if report['auto_fail']:

   745→            elif report['score'] < THRESHOLDS['commit']:

   748→        except Exception as e:

   750→            import traceback

   752→            exit_code = 1
   753→
   754→    if args.json:

   757→    sys.exit(exit_code)

     8→
     9→# Literature Review
    10→
    11→Conduct a structured literature search and synthesis on the given topic.
    12→
    13→**Input:** `$ARGUMENTS` — a topic, paper title, research question, or phenomenon to investigate.
    14→
    15→---
    16→
    17→## Steps
    18→
    19→1. **Parse the topic** from `$ARGUMENTS`. If a specific paper is named, use it as the anchor.
    20→
    21→2. **Search for related work** using available tools:
    22→   - Check `master_supporting_docs/supporting_papers/` for uploaded papers
    23→   - Use `WebSearch` to find recent publications (if available)
    24→   - Use `WebFetch` to access working paper repositories (if available)
    25→   - Read any existing `.bib` file for papers already in the project
    26→
    27→3. **Organize findings** into these categories:
    28→   - **Theoretical contributions** — models, frameworks, mechanisms

0→#!/bin/bash
     1→# sync_to_docs.sh
     2→# Renders Quarto slides and syncs everything to docs/ for GitHub Pages
     3→#
     4→# Usage: ./scripts/sync_to_docs.sh [lecture_name]
     5→# Examples:
     6→#   ./scripts/sync_to_docs.sh                    # Sync all lectures
     7→#   ./scripts/sync_to_docs.sh Lecture2           # Sync only Lecture2
     8→
     9→set -e
    10→
    11→REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
    12→QUARTO_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/Quarto"
    13→DOCS_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/docs"
    14→
    15→echo "=== Syncing Quarto slides to docs/ ==="
    16→echo "Repo root: $REPO_ROOT"
    17→
    18→# 1. Render Quarto files
    19→cd "$QUARTO_DIR"
    20→
    21→if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    22→    # Render specific lecture
    23→    echo "Rendering $1..."
    24→    matched_qmd=$(ls ${1}_*.qmd ${1}.qmd 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    25→    if [ -n "$matched_qmd" ]; then
    26→        quarto render "$matched_qmd"
    27→    else
    28→        echo "Error: No QMD file found matching '${1}'"
    29→        exit 1
    30→    fi
    31→else
    32→    # Render all QMD files (skip backups)
    33→    echo "Rendering all Quarto files..."
    34→    for qmd in *.qmd; do
    35→        if [ -f "$qmd" ] && [[ ! "$qmd" == *"_backup"* ]]; then
    36→            echo "  Rendering $qmd..."
    37→            quarto render "$qmd" || echo "  Warning: Failed to render $qmd"
    38→        fi
    39→    done
    40→fi
    41→
    42→# 2. Sync HTML files and their _files directories to docs/slides/
    43→echo "Syncing HTML and assets to docs/slides/..."
    44→mkdir -p "$DOCS_DIR/slides"
    45→
    46→for html in *.html; do
    47→    if [ -f "$html" ]; then
    48→        echo "  Copying $html..."
    49→        cp "$html" "$DOCS_DIR/slides/"
    50→
    51→        # Copy associated _files directory if it exists
    52→        files_dir="${html%.html}_files"
    53→        if [ -d "$files_dir" ]; then
    54→            echo "  Copying $files_dir/..."
    55→            rm -rf "$DOCS_DIR/slides/$files_dir"
    56→            cp -r "$files_dir" "$DOCS_DIR/slides/"
    57→        fi
    58→    fi
    59→done
    60→
    61→# 3. Sync Beamer PDFs to docs/slides/
    62→echo "Syncing Beamer PDFs..."
    63→for pdf in "$REPO_ROOT/Slides/"*.pdf; do
    64→    if [ -f "$pdf" ]; then
    65→        echo "  Copying $(basename "$pdf")..."
    66→        cp "$pdf" "$DOCS_DIR/slides/"
    67→    fi
    68→done
    69→
    70→# 4. Sync R scripts to docs/files/code/
    71→echo "Syncing R scripts..."
    72→mkdir -p "$DOCS_DIR/files/code"
    73→for rscript in "$REPO_ROOT/scripts/R/"*.R; do
    74→    if [ -f "$rscript" ]; then
    75→        echo "  Copying $(basename "$rscript")..."
    76→        cp "$rscript" "$DOCS_DIR/files/code/"
    77→    fi
    78→done
    79→
    80→# 5. Sync Figures directory (using rsync for efficiency)
    81→echo "Syncing Figures/..."
    82→if command -v rsync &> /dev/null; then
    83→    rsync -av --delete "$REPO_ROOT/Figures/" "$DOCS_DIR/Figures/"
    84→else
    85→    rm -rf "$DOCS_DIR/Figures"
    86→    cp -r "$REPO_ROOT/Figures" "$DOCS_DIR/Figures"
    87→fi
    88→
    89→echo ""
    90→echo "=== Sync complete! ==="
    91→echo "Files synced to: $DOCS_DIR/slides/"
    92→

- LaTeX citation extraction (used for cross-checking with .bib):
  - Regex used: \\cite[a-z]*\{([^}]+)\}
  - Function: IssueDetector.check_broken_citations(content, bib_file)
- Quarto-style citation extraction (used for @key patterns):
  - Bracketed pattern: r'\[([^\]]*@[^\]]+)\]'
  - Standalone @-pattern: r'(?<![.\w])@([\w:.#$%&\-+?<>~/]+)'
  - Function: IssueDetector.check_quarto_citations(content, bib_file)
These functions already implement:
  - extraction of citation keys from .qmd and .tex sources,
  - comparison against Bibliography_base.bib (regex to collect bib keys: r'@\w+\{([^,]+),').

Why these are useful
- A Google Scholar XML → Markdown/BibTeX converter needs to:
  1. parse the XML export to extract fields (title, authors, year, venue, URL, abstract, citation key),
  2. generate a Markdown summary (or multiple entry Markdown files) and/or a BibTeX entry,
  3. cross-check produced BibTeX keys against the repository bibliography and lecture citations.
- The repository already has robust citation-extraction and cross-check patterns you can reuse for step 3 (matching keys and reporting missing/unused entries).
- The templates and skills (validate-bib, lit-review) document the desired output locations and formats.

Where to integrate a parser (recommended locations / conventions)
- Implementation script (suggested, do not create yet): scripts/scholar_import.py
  - Parse Google Scholar XML and produce:
    - Markdown summaries saved to quality_reports/scholar_imports/<sanitized-author-year>.md
    - BibTeX entries appended (or suggested) to Bibliography_base.bib (repo root)
  - Use the same citation key naming convention used in Bibliography_base.bib (AuthorYear_keyword)
- Output locations consistent with repo governance:
  - derived Markdown / summaries → quality_reports/ (or quality_reports/scholar_imports/)
  - generated BibTeX entries → Bibliography_base.bib (or a sub-bib in output/ then merged)
  - any helper logs or reports → quality_reports/
- Render / sync flow: after generating Markdown/QMD/BibTeX, use existing scripts/sync_to_docs.sh to render and push to docs/ (if needed).

Concrete next steps (recommended, brief)
1. Implement parsing of Google Scholar XML:
   - parse items, authors, year, venue, URL, abstract, and citation count
   - map to a clean BibTeX record (choose entry type: @article/@inproceedings/@misc)
   - create a sanitized citation key following the repo convention (e.g., AuthorYYYY_keyword)
2. Produce Markdown summary per item:
   - minimal front matter (title, authors, year, source, URL)
   - one-paragraph summary + BibTeX block
   - save into quality_reports/scholar_imports/
3. Reuse quality_score.py's citation-extraction regexes to:
   - validate the new keys against Bibliography_base.bib
   - detect collisions or similar-but-not-identical keys
4. Run the .claude/skills/validate-bib workflow to produce a cross-reference report.

Notes & caveats
- No existing Google Scholar XML parser code was found in the repository; you will need to add a new script.
- Follow the repository governance: never overwrite files in papers/, store derived outputs under quality_reports/ or output/, and use Bibliography_base.bib for master bib entries.
- The quality_score.py functions and .claude/skills/validate-bib provide ready-made patterns for citation extraction and verification — reuse these to avoid reinventing regexes and report formats.

If you want, I can:
- Draft a small implementation plan (files to add, functions to write, example XML→BibTeX mapping) — note this is multi-file / non-trivial so Plan-First would be appropriate per repo rules; or
- Produce a minimal example Python function (pseudo-code) that parses a single Google Scholar XML entry and emits both a Markdown snippet and a BibTeX entry (read-only preview only, no file writes).

# Context
- Need to summarize four Google Scholar XML exports (MISQ, JMIS, ISR, MS) into a single Markdown report grouped by the agreed taxonomy.
- Source XML files are read-only; derived artifacts must live under `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/` per governance rules.
- Deduplication using DOI/title and keyword-based classification must be documented thoroughly for reproducibility.

# Files Involved
- Source XMLs (read-only):
  - `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/scholar-export-2026-03-20 MISQ.xml`
  - `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/scholar-export-2026-03-20 JMIS.xml`
  - `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/scholar-export-2026-03-20 ISR.xml`
  - `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/scholar-export-2026-03-20 MS.xml`
- New assets to create/modify:
  - `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/scripts/parse_scholar_exports.py` (parsing, normalization, dedup, classification, Markdown generation)
  - `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/organized-scholar-entries.md` (final grouped summary)
  - `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/tmp/duplicates_report.csv` (optional log for transparency)

# Constraints
- Do not edit the XML exports.
- Deduplicate entries by DOI (case-insensitive); when DOI is missing, normalize titles (lowercase, trim whitespace/punctuation) to identify duplicates.
- Preserve per-topic counts, note multi-topic overlaps, and flag missing DOI/PDF links in verification block.
- Use defined keyword triggers per topic; unmatched entries go under "Other / Uncategorized." If entry spans multiple topics, include it in each section with an "also listed under" note.
- All outputs (Markdown, script, logs) must remain within `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/`.

# Proposed Plan
1. Build a Python script at `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/scripts/parse_scholar_exports.py` that:
   - Reads each input XML as an Excel Spreadsheet 2003 document, maps headers to fields (Title, Authors, Journal, Year, Abstract/Notes, DOI/Open URL, PDF URL, etc.), and counts rows per file.
   - Normalizes content by trimming whitespace, collapsing abstract whitespace, standardizing DOI (include https://doi.org/ prefix when missing), and defining a primary URL (priority: DOI link → PDF URL → open-access URL).
   - Captures metadata (source file, row index) for each row to support the appendix and dedup reporting.
   - Deduplicates entries using a map keyed by DOI when available, else by normalized title, recording which raw entries collapsed for the verification block and optional duplicates log.
   - Applies keyword-trigger classification (case-insensitive over title + abstract) across the five topic buckets and tracks per-topic counts, multi-topic overlaps, and missing URL stats.
   - Selects five representative entries for the spot-check table (ensure they cover different topics) and records the keywords that triggered their classifications.
   - Writes `organized-scholar-entries.md` with:
     - Metadata header: date, per-file raw counts, total raw vs unique entries, dedup strategy, keyword rules, per-topic tallies, multi-topic count, missing DOI/PDF count.
     - Verification block summarizing counts and includes spot-check table (Title, source file/row, topic, triggering keywords).
     - Sections per topic (Website Experience & Design; AI / Automation-Driven Systems; Consumer / Trust Signals; Visual / Multimedia Interactions; Other / Uncategorized) with a short description and keyword list, followed by bullet entries formatted as: `- Title (primary link) — Authors. Journal (Year). DOI/PDF/Open link.` Extended info (notes/abstract, PDF) goes on the following indented lines. Multi-topic entries get `(also listed under: ...)` annotations.
     - Appendix listing canonical entries (one per unique record) with source file and row reference for traceability.
     - Verification block at top (raw counts, dedup summary, missing links, spot-check table).
   - Optionally saves duplicates log at `/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting/tmp/duplicates_report.csv` showing which rows were collapsed and why.
2. Run the script to produce `organized-scholar-entries.md`, ensuring Markdown conforms to the taxonomy and includes verification data.
3. Double-check that raw row counts match XML tables (e.g., script prints totals), unique counts reflect deduplication, per-topic tallies align with classification results, and the appendix references the original file/row for each entry.

# Classification Keywords (per instructions)
- Website Experience & Design → website, webpage, ux, "user experience", navigation, interface, layout, mobile, responsive, homepage, design
- AI / Automation-Driven Systems → ai, "machine learning", algorithm, automation, recommender, personalization, bot, "automated", nlp, "deep learning"
- Consumer / Trust Signals → trust, privacy, disclosure, reputation, review, rating, credibility, signal, "trustworthy", "information disclosure"
- Visual / Multimedia Interactions → image, video, visual, multimedia, ar, vr, augmented, 3d, infographic, visualisation, visualization
- Other / Uncategorized → entries matching none of the above
Matching is case-insensitive and performed on both Title and Abstract/Notes.

# Verification Plan
- Log raw row counts per XML export immediately after parsing to ensure all rows are read.
- Compare total raw rows vs deduplicated unique entries; include both numbers in the metadata header and verification block.
- Track counts per topic and number of entries assigned to multiple topics; include in verification block with multi-topic count.
- Record how many entries lack DOI and/or PDF links and mention in verification block (flag for missing links).
- Generate spot-check table (five varied entries) showing source file/row and the keywords that triggered each topic assignment.
- Ensure appendix lists each canonical entry along with its source file/row reference for traceability.

# Notes
- This task introduces a new automation script and derived Markdown summary; keep plan-first principles by saving documentation and verification outputs (e.g., duplicates log) under the RA/Junting directory structure.
- After implementing and verifying counts, return here and exit plan mode with a summary.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Parse Google Scholar XML exports (Excel Spreadsheet 2003 format) and generate
an organized Markdown summary grouped by research topic with deduplication.

Author: Claude (Anthropic)
"""

import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
import re
import random
from pathlib import Path
from collections import defaultdict
from datetime import datetime

# Configuration
INPUT_DIR = Path("/Users/my/Desktop/RA/Junting")
OUTPUT_FILE = INPUT_DIR / "organized-scholar-entries.md"
DUPLICATES_LOG = INPUT_DIR / "tmp/duplicates_report.csv"

# Topic classification keywords (case-insensitive)
TOPIC_KEYWORDS = {
    "Website Experience & Design": [
        "website", "webpage", "ux", "user experience", "navigation",
        "interface", "layout", "mobile", "responsive", "homepage", "design"
    ],
    "AI / Automation-Driven Systems": [
        "ai", "machine learning", "algorithm", "automation", "recommender",
        "personalization", "bot", "automated", "nlp", "deep learning"
    ],
    "Consumer / Trust Signals": [
        "trust", "privacy", "disclosure", "reputation", "review",
        "rating", "credibility", "signal", "trustworthy", "information disclosure"
    ],
    "Visual / Multimedia Interactions": [
        "image", "video", "visual", "multimedia", "ar", "vr",
        "augmented", "3d", "infographic", "visualisation", "visualization"
    ],
    # "Other / Uncategorized" is implicit fallback
}

# XML namespaces
NS = {"ss": "urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:spreadsheet"}


def parse_xml_file(filepath):
    """Parse Excel XML Spreadsheet 2003 format and extract entries."""
    tree = ET.parse(filepath)
    root = tree.getroot()

    # Find the first worksheet and its table
    worksheet = root.find(".//ss:Worksheet", NS)
    if worksheet is None:
        return [], []

    table = worksheet.find("ss:Table", NS)
    if table is None:
        return [], []

    rows = table.findall("ss:Row", NS)
    if len(rows) < 2:
        return [], []

    # First row is header
    header_row = rows[0]
    headers = []
    for cell in header_row.findall("ss:Cell", NS):
        data = cell.find("ss:Data", NS)
        headers.append(data.text.strip() if data is not None and data.text else "")

    # Extract data rows
    entries = []
    for row_idx, row in enumerate(rows[1:], start=2):  # row 1 is header, data starts at 2
        cells = row.findall("ss:Cell", NS)
        entry = {"_source_file": filepath.name, "_source_row": row_idx}

        # Map cell values to headers
        for i, cell in enumerate(cells):
            if i < len(headers):
                data = cell.find("ss:Data", NS)
                value = data.text.strip() if data is not None and data.text else ""
                entry[headers[i]] = value

        entries.append(entry)

    return headers, entries


def normalize_doi(doi_str):
    """Normalize DOI to lowercase and ensure consistent format."""
    if not doi_str:
        return None
    doi_str = doi_str.strip().lower()
    # Extract DOI from URL if needed
    if "doi.org/" in doi_str:
        match = re.search(r"doi\.org/(.+)", doi_str)
        if match:
            return match.group(1).rstrip("/")
    if doi_str.startswith("10."):
        return doi_str
    return None


def normalize_title(title):
    """Normalize title for deduplication (lowercase, remove punctuation)."""
    if not title:
        return ""
    title = title.lower().strip()
    # Remove punctuation and collapse whitespace
    title = re.sub(r"[^\w\s]", " ", title)
    title = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", title)
    return title


def get_primary_url(entry):
    """Get primary URL in priority order: DOI > PDF > Open URL."""
    doi = entry.get("DOI", "") or entry.get("Open-access URL", "")
    pdf_url = entry.get("PDF URL", "")

    # Normalize DOI
    norm_doi = normalize_doi(doi)
    if norm_doi:
        return f"https://doi.org/{norm_doi}"

    if pdf_url:
        return pdf_url

    if doi:
        return doi

    return ""


def classify_entry(entry):
    """Classify entry into topics based on keyword matching."""
    title = entry.get("Title", "") or ""
    abstract = entry.get("Abstract", "") or entry.get("Notes", "") or ""
    combined_text = (title + " " + abstract).lower()

    matched_topics = []

    for topic, keywords in TOPIC_KEYWORDS.items():
        for keyword in keywords:
            # Handle multi-word phrases
            pattern = r"\b" + re.escape(keyword.lower()) + r"\b"
            if re.search(pattern, combined_text):
                matched_topics.append(topic)
                break

    if not matched_topics:
        matched_topics.append("Other / Uncategorized")

    return matched_topics


def find_triggered_keywords(entry, topic):
    """Find which keywords triggered a topic for an entry."""
    title = entry.get("Title", "") or ""

    print("\nGenerating spot-check entries...")
    spot_checks = []

    # Try to get one entry from each topic for variety
    available_topics = [t for t in entries_by_topic if entries_by_topic[t]]

    for topic in available_topics[:5]:

    while len(sampled_entries) < 5 and len(unique_entries) > len(sampled_entries):

    for entry in sampled_entries[:5]:
        topics = entry.get("_topics", [])
        # Get triggering keywords for first topic
        triggered = []
        for topic in topics:

            "title": entry.get("Title", ""),

    metadata = {

        "total_unique": len(unique_entries),
        "duplicates_removed": len(duplicates),
        "missing_links": missing_links,
        "multi_topic_count": multi_topic_count,
    }

    # Generate Markdown
    print("\nGenerating Markdown output...")
    markdown = generate_markdown(entries_by_topic, metadata, spot_checks)

    # Write output
    OUTPUT_FILE.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with open(OUTPUT_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:

    print(f"\nOutput written to: {OUTPUT_FILE}")

    # Save duplicates log
    if duplicates:

    if worksheet is None:

    table = worksheet.find("ss:Table", NS)

    rows = table.findall("ss:Row", NS)

    """Main entry point."""
    xml_files = sorted(INPUT_DIR.glob("scholar-export-*.xml"))

    if not xml_files:
        print(f"No XML files found in {INPUT_DIR}")
        return

    print(f"Found {len(xml_files)} XML files to process")

    # Parse all files
    all_entries = []
    raw_counts = {}

    for xml_file in xml_files:
        print(f"Parsing {xml_file.name}...")
        headers, entries = parse_xml_file(xml_file)
        print(f"  - Found {len(entries)} entries with headers: {headers}")
        all_entries.extend(entries)
        raw_counts[xml_file.name] = len(entries)

    print(f"\nTotal raw entries: {len(all_entries)}")

    # Deduplicate
    print("\nDeduplicating entries...")
    unique_entries, duplicates = deduplicate_entries(all_entries)
    print(f"  - Unique entries: {len(unique_entries)}")
    print(f"  - Duplicates removed: {len(duplicates)}")

    # Classify entries
    print("\nClassifying entries...")
    entries_by_topic = defaultdict(list)
    multi_topic_count = 0
    missing_links = 0

    for entry in unique_entries:
        topics = classify_entry(entry)
        entry["_topics"] = topics

        if len(topics) > 1:
            multi_topic_count += 1

        # Check for missing links
        primary_url = get_primary_url(entry)
        if not primary_url:
            missing_links += 1

        for topic in topics:
            entries_by_topic[topic].append(entry)

    print(f"  - Multi-topic entries: {multi_topic_count}")
    print(f"  - Entries missing DOI/PDF: {missing_links}")

    # Per-topic counts
    print("\nTopic distribution:")
    for topic in list(TOPIC_KEYWORDS.keys()) + ["Other / Uncategorized"]:
        count = len(entries_by_topic.get(topic, []))
        print(f"  - {topic}: {count}")

    # Generate spot-check entries (5 random, different topics if possible)
    print("\nGenerating spot-check entries...")
    spot_checks = []

    # Try to get one entry from each topic for variety
    available_topics = [t for t in entries_by_topic if entries_by_topic[t]]
    sampled_entries = []

    for topic in available_topics[:5]:
        entries = entries_by_topic[topic]
        if entries:
            sampled_entries.append(random.choice(entries))

    # If we need more, sample randomly
    while len(sampled_entries) < 5 and len(unique_entries) > len(sampled_entries):
        candidate = random.choice(unique_entries)
        if candidate not in sampled_entries:
            sampled_entries.append(candidate)

    for entry in sampled_entries[:5]:
        topics = entry.get("_topics", [])
        # Get triggering keywords for first topic
        triggered = []
        for topic in topics:
            triggered.extend(find_triggered_keywords(entry, topic))

        spot_checks.append({
            "title": entry.get("Title", ""),
            "source_file": entry.get("_source_file", ""),
            "source_row": entry.get("_source_row", "?"),
            "topics": topics,
            "keywords": triggered,
        })

    # Metadata
    metadata = {
        "raw_counts": raw_counts,
        "total_raw": len(all_entries),
        "total_unique": len(unique_entries),
        "duplicates_removed": len(duplicates),
        "missing_links": missing_links,
        "multi_topic_count": multi_topic_count,
    }

    # Generate Markdown
    print("\nGenerating Markdown output...")
    markdown = generate_markdown(entries_by_topic, metadata, spot_checks)

    # Write output
    OUTPUT_FILE.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with open(OUTPUT_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(markdown)

    print(f"\nOutput written to: {OUTPUT_FILE}")

    # Save duplicates log
    if duplicates:
        save_duplicates_log(duplicates, DUPLICATES_LOG)
        print(f"Duplicates log saved to: {DUPLICATES_LOG}")

    print("\nDone!")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

Notes about behavior and choices
- Deduplication:
  - Primary key: DOI (case-insensitive, extracted from DOI column or URL containing doi.org).
  - Fallback key: Normalized title (lowercased, punctuation removed, whitespace collapsed).
  - I recorded which rows were collapsed into duplicates_report.csv for traceability.
- URL selection priority:
  - DOI link (https://doi.org/...) → PDF URL → Open-access URL (if present).
- Classification:
  - Keyword-based matching (case-insensitive) across Title + Abstract/Notes fields.
  - If an entry matches multiple topic keyword lists, it is included in each matched topic and annotated with (also listed under: ...).
  - If no keywords matched, the entry is placed under Other / Uncategorized.
- Spot-check:
  - The report contains a spot-check table with five sampled entries showing title, source file/row, assigned topic(s), and the keywords that triggered each assignment.
